events.label: tool result
events.text: File created successfully at: /app/answer.json (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

events.label: assistant
events.text: Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. Bottom line: a pneumatic test running with no exclusion zone is a stop-work, critical-risk violation under ASME B31.3 §345 — the standard requires a documented hazard review (favoring hydrostatic where practical) and, if pneumatic is used, an exclusion zone with restricted access and remote monitoring before pressurization proceeds. Work must halt, depressurize, and the exclusion zone/controls must be verified before any retest.
